I have attached an old version, from a different project, of the code that I used to back anthology data into a binary form. I tried to remember the examine the code to outline what exactly the logic does, however I will need you to consider the example I have below along with the logic to determine a aplicable version for storing the anthology data in the build.json as a storage save state. (For the sake up simplification I have removed the 'SAMRAS' datums and simplified the anthology). The logic is pretty straight forward, the only complicated part is how the code knows a value is where and what. As for the example below, the only caviatte is the use of VG-2 instances, this can be understoof from the prior portions of encoding, but it is a different aproach in term that only deffine how many tuples of reffefrence (by order) and then magnitude there are, since we know which datums are what value groups this is how mutliple tuples are understood to be a part of the same datum. (One other facet worth noting is that the reffernce value might have been implemented differently for tuples in the example below vs the code, regaudless this requires a necissary use of a COBM inbetween each layer to deffine which datums are used going forward, this allow for the size of 'refferences' to always be known for a given layer by always alloting the size of the reffernce value to be the maximum size of datums that could be reffernced with binary. However in this example we just used reffernce values that refferences the prior datums via order that they came, and ultimelty this would not work in binary sequnce as there wouldn't be any way of knowing where the refference ends and the magnitude begins. Note for later.)

indexa: how many bit indexes is an address
indexb: the size of the array

Example starting with the single bit sequence:
